h2h_summary: Canada lead the all-time head-to-head with 2 wins from 2 meetings, including a 3-1 friendly win in 2002 and a comfortable 2-0 victory over Qatar on neutral ground in September 2022.
key_players_home: Alphonso Davies (left back / winger, captain, Bayern Munich)
Jonathan David (striker, Juventus, Canada all-time leading scorer)
Stephen Eustaquio (central midfielder, FC Porto, vice-captain)
key_players_away: Akram Afif (winger / attacking midfielder, Al-Sadd, two-time Asian Player of the Year)
Almoez Ali (striker, Al-Duhail, top scorer in AFC World Cup qualifying)
Hassan Al-Haydos (attacking midfielder, Al Sadd, captain)
Mohammed Muntari (striker, Al Gharafa, Qatar's only 2022 WC goalscorer)
Assim Madibo (defensive midfielder, double pivot anchor)
team_style_home: Canada under Jesse Marsch employ an aggressive high-press 4-4-2 / 4-2-3-1 system known as 'Maplepressing', winning the ball high and launching rapid vertical attacks through pacey wide players and a dynamic midfield double pivot.
team_style_away: Qatar under Julen Lopetegui use a disciplined 4-2-3-1 / 4-3-3 built on compact defensive blocks, a double-pivot midfield, and quick transitions that funnel the ball into Akram Afif in dangerous half-spaces.
wc_history_home: Canada have appeared at the World Cup twice (1986 and 2022), failing to win a single match on both occasions, though they recorded their historic first World Cup goal through Alphonso Davies against Croatia in 2022.
wc_history_away: Qatar participated in the 2022 World Cup as hosts but became the first host nation to lose all three group games, finishing bottom of Group A without a point.
group_context: Group B features co-hosts Canada, Switzerland (the group favourites), Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Qatar; Switzerland and Canada are widely projected to advance to the knockout rounds, making the Canada vs Qatar match a must-win for Qatar and an important chance for Canada to build momentum on home soil.
strengths_home: High-intensity pressing and rapid transitions exploit defensive errors
World-class individual quality in Alphonso Davies and Jonathan David
Massive home crowd advantage at BC Place in Vancouver
strengths_away: Reigning two-time AFC Asian Cup champions with tournament experience
Creative danger through Akram Afif in tight spaces
Experienced squad coached by tactically sophisticated Julen Lopetegui
